Our experienced technicians eliminate hornet nests and prevent future infestations, protecting your family and property from these dangerous stinging insects.

Our licensed professionals understand the unique pest challenges of Staten Island homes and offer same-day service with guaranteed results and competitive pricing from $350-$550.
localContext: Graniteville's mix of residential homes, the sprawling LaTourette Park and Golf Course, and industrial areas along the West Shore create ideal nesting conditions for hornets in eaves, trees, and outbuildings. Our team is familiar with the neighborhood's architecture from older single-family homes near the historic Richmond County Country Club to newer developments, ensuring effective hornet removal tailored to your property's specific needs.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in Graniteville?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day hornet removal service in Graniteville depending on availability. Most hornet nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, and we'll safely eliminate the threat and remove the nest to prevent hornets from returning to the site.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 hornet removal service?
faqs.answer: Our hornet removal service includes a thorough property inspection, safe nest elimination using professional-grade products, nest removal when accessible, treatment of the surrounding area to deter returning hornets, and a follow-up warranty. The final cost depends on nest size, location, and accessibility.

faqs.question: Are hornet removal treatments safe for my family and pets in Graniteville?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products and proven techniques that are safe for children and pets when applied by our trained professionals. We'll provide clear instructions on any brief precautions needed during treatment, and you can typically resume normal activities within a few hours.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have hornets versus wasps or bees?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than wasps (up to 2 inches), have thicker bodies, and build large, enclosed paper nests often in trees or under eaves. They're more aggressive than bees and can sting multiple times. If you're unsure, our Graniteville technicians will identify the species during inspection and recommend the appropriate removal strategy.
